title: Empty `NetworkSettings` when doing `podman inspect` on container using pasta
body:
### Issue Description

After updating Podman from 5.0.0 to 5.0.2 on CentOS 9 Stream, we were suddenly hit with the issue of default rootless containers not being able to connect to ports on the host IP. IIUC this shouldn't have worked in 5.0.0 already because of the switch to pasta as the default network provider for rootless containers, but somehow it did :man_shrugging: 

Now apart from the fact that this breaking change was poorly communicated and documented in the release notes, this issue was very hard to debug as `podman inspect <container>` doesn't give any network information at all. Yes, there is `"NetworkMode": "pasta",`, but that's it. The network settings however are completely empty, which left me puzzled about the IP of the container.

Would it be possible to populate this with any information for containers using pasta? Even if it was just the same settings as used on the host, it would have helped me. It was also very confusing, because older posts like [this](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-ip-address-podman) don't even mention pasta.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run rootless container with pasta networking, e.g. `podman run --rm -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test postgres:14.12-alpine`
2. Inspect it, e.g. `podman inspect sharp_mendeleev | jq '.[0].NetworkSettings'`


### Describe the results you received

Empty details:
```json
          "NetworkSettings": {
               "EndpointID": "",
               "Gateway": "",
               "IPAddress": "",
               "IPPrefixLen": 0,
               "IPv6Gateway": "",
               "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
               "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "MacAddress": "",
               "Bridge": "",
               "SandboxID": "",
               "HairpinMode": false,
               "LinkLocalIPv6Address": "",
               "LinkLocalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "Ports": {},
               "SandboxKey": "/run/user/1001/netns/netns-896fcf14-d119-7aa4-7703-fc9df3646f14"
          },
```

### Describe the results you expected

More information, maybe put networking mode here instead of in the HostConfig? Or at least put the host IP that's being used here.

### podman info output

```yaml

    - container_id: 1

      size: 65536
  kernel: 6.8.9-300.fc40.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 5720895488

  networkBackend: netavark

    backend: netavark
    dns:
      package: aardvark-dns-1.10.0-1.fc40.x86_64
```

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

comments.body:
What purpose would the ip serve? You cannot connect to the ip from the host or other containers as they are in separate namespaces and there is no directly routable connection between them. Showing the ip would just confuse people even more IMO. 

The inspect output is inherited from docker and almost all of these fields do not make sense for slirp4netns/pasta so we just leave them empty. We do set SandboxKey and Ports so they are also not all empty. The SandboxKey is useful to join the netns. I guess we could also fill in the MacAddress.

> Now apart from the fact that this breaking change was poorly communicated and documented in the release notes

How and where should we communicate such changes, there is https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/podman-5-0-breaking-changes-in-detail/ that goes into more detail.

> It was also very confusing, because older posts like [this](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-ip-address-podman) don't even mention pasta.

Well the blog post is over two years old, back then pasta integration didn't even exists nor is this a official podman documentation. (I know our docs are also far from perfect)

> How and where should we communicate such changes, there is [blog.podman.io/2024/03/podman-5-0-breaking-changes-in-detail](https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/podman-5-0-breaking-changes-in-detail/) that goes into more detail.

In the release notes for 5.0.0 it only states this:
> The default tool for rootless networking has been swapped from slirp4netns to pasta for improved performance. As a result, networks named pasta are no longer supported.

A link to the blog post can nowhere be found in the release, this or at least some hints about the implications of this change would have been nice. The mention of networks named pasta would suggest that this is the _only_ important result. In general I'd say you can't expect people to google for blog posts every time there is a new release, just put it at the top of the release notes (even though that makes automatic release processes more complicated).

> What purpose would the ip serve? You cannot connect to the ip from the host or other containers as they are in separate namespaces and there is no directly routable connection between them. Showing the ip would just confuse people even more IMO.
> 
> The inspect output is inherited from docker and almost all of these fields do not make sense for slirp4netns/pasta so we just leave them empty. We do set SandboxKey and Ports so they are also not all empty. The SandboxKey is useful to join the netns. I guess we could also fill in the MacAddress.

I see. I guess the Docker compatibility is the issue (but it's needed, I know), as it prevents podman from showing "only useful" information here. If you think this doesn't make sense, feel free to close this issue, at least others can find it then if someone has the same thought as I am. 

I am probably just frustrated because I wasted 2 hours debugging and fixing this (+30 mins because the `[network]` line was missing in the second pasta config example in the blog post and I forgot to add it) while our whole CI blew up because of this change. I wanted to get the IP of the container to get an idea why this suddenly stopped working and I just couldn't. Maybe it's just me, but debugging networking issues in podman can be a real pain because most images are missing tools like `ip`.
(While debugging I tried to run our containers with `--network=bridge`, only to notice that DNS resolution doesn't work. I understand that many of these limitations have their reasons, but sometimes you just sit there thinking _why on earth doesn't this just work_?)

> > How and where should we communicate such changes, there is [blog.podman.io/2024/03/podman-5-0-breaking-changes-in-detail](https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/podman-5-0-breaking-changes-in-detail/) that goes into more detail.
> 
> In the release notes for 5.0.0 it only states this:
> 
> > The default tool for rootless networking has been swapped from slirp4netns to pasta for improved performance. As a result, networks named pasta are no longer supported.
> 
> A link to the blog post can nowhere be found in the release, this or at least some hints about the implications of this change would have been nice. The mention of networks named pasta would suggest that this is the _only_ important result. In general I'd say you can't expect people to google for blog posts every time there is a new release, just put it at the top of the release notes (even though that makes automatic release processes more complicated).

Thanks, to be clear my comment was not meant in any way negative. I just like to know so I can improve this going forward. Linking the blogs in the releases notes is definitely something we can and should do. Writing releases notes is hard and complicated, some want a short log, some want more details, etc... We didn't anticipate the amount of "breaking" scenarios with pasta so we did not consider this such a big deal. In hindsight this should have been made much more clear to users I agree.


> Maybe it's just me, but debugging networking issues in podman can be a real pain because most images are missing tools like ip.

You don't need networking tools inside the container
```
podman unshare nsenter -n$(podman inspect --format '{{.NetworkSettings.SandboxKey}}' c1) ip a
```
or when running as root remove the `podman unshare`, you can execute any host command in the container netns like this.

> I am probably just frustrated because I wasted 2 hours debugging and fixing this (+30 mins because the [network] line was missing in the second pasta config example in the blog post and I forgot to add it)

I edited the post and added the `[network]` line, hopefully it helps others




---

> > The inspect output is inherited from docker and almost all of these fields do not make sense for slirp4netns/pasta so we just leave them empty. We do set SandboxKey and Ports so they are also not all empty. The SandboxKey is useful to join the netns. I guess we could also fill in the MacAddress.
> 
> I see. I guess the Docker compatibility is the issue (but it's needed, I know), as it prevents podman from showing "only useful" information here. If you think this doesn't make sense, feel free to close this issue, at least others can find it then if someone has the same thought as I am.

It depends, there is no formal of course. I think adding the ip there could confuse others as they try to use it for connections. And it is hard to tell the impact of such change, thus I rather leave the behavior as is unless there are strong arguments for doing it.
I'd agree with your arguing. Thanks for the elaborate answers and your time!
